mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW M4 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW M4 engine typically costs £7,000–£10,000+ supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — the S55 and S58 both cost in this range. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW M4 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S58B30 in the G82 — widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern performance engine. The S55B30 in the F82 is proven but carries the crank hub caveat.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW M4 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but a modified S55 without a crank hub fix is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The crank hub is the Achilles heel of the S55. A G82 with S58 has no such caveat.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my M4 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: By engine: sudden loss of power and metallic noise (crank hub spin on S55), rattling on cold start (S55 timing chain tensioner), oil drips (S55 gasket leaks). On the S55, any unusual engine noise at high RPM is a warning sign. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £7,000–£10,000+ engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW M4 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M4's values are strong enough to justify engine work. Against a £30,000 F82, a £7,000–£10,000+ S55 recon is 23–33% — ✅ safe. Against a £60,000 G82, a £7,000–£10,000+ S58 recon is 12–17% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Use our calculator for your car.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW M4 should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: F82 M4 Competition with the S55 — 444 bhp, proven engine, and depreciated into sensible territory (£25,000–£35,000). Budget for a crank hub fix (£1,500–£3,000) or buy one that's already been done. A 2017+ facelift car with the Competition package is the one to buy. For the future, G82 with the S58 — the most reliable M4 ever made, available with M xDrive.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW M4 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your generation and variant (F82, F83, G82, G83) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: BMW M4 vs M3 — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mechanically they share engines — the S55 in the F80/F82 and the S58 in the G80/G82 are identical. The M3 has four doors; the M4 has two. Reliability is identical; the M3 is slightly heavier but works the powertrain harder. Buy on engine and year, not badge. The M3 offers more practicality; the M4 offers more style.

hero.tagPill: BMW M4 • 2014–Present • 2 Generations (F82/F83, G82/G83) • S55, S58
hero.h1: M4 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW M4 engine across two generations — from the S55 twin-turbo straight-six of the F82 to the S58 in the G82. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's performance coupe. Backed by real UK M4 engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The M4 rule — the coupe premium: an S55 recon at £7,000–£10,000+ against a £30,000–£40,000 F82 is 18–33% — ✅ safe on most examples. Against a £60,000 G82, an S58 recon at £7,000–£10,000+ is 12–17% — ✅ safe. The M4's values are strong enough to justify engine work.
★★★★☆ — the S55 is proven but the crank hub is the Achilles heel; the S58 is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern performance engine
S58B30 (G82) — the latest M engine is proving exceptionally robust
S55B30 (F82) — crank hub spin (especially on tuned cars)
F82 M4 Competition with S55 — 444 bhp, proven engine, depreciated, and the sweet spot in the range
G82 M4 Competition with S58 — 503 bhp, xDrive available, the most reliable M engine
🏆 The Performance Coupe — the M3's two-door sibling with the same engine DNA

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The M4 is the M3's coupe sibling — the same engines, the same risks, and the same rewards. The S55 is proven but the crank hub is the Achilles heel; the S58 is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern performance engine. Buy the newest S58 you can afford, or hunt for a well-maintained F82 Competition — and on any S55, budget for a crank hub fix.

rows.value: H2: BMW M4 Generations — Find Your Vehicle

rows.metric: Sub-headline: Two generations, two body styles, and one mission — to be the ultimate performance coupe. The right M4 depends on your budget and which engine you want.
BMW M4 F82/F83 🔥 Most Enquired — S55 drives volume [BMW-VERIFIED]

rows.value: Our Verdict: The original M4 — the coupe and convertible that replaced the E92 M3. The S55 is proven and powerful, but the crank hub is the Achilles heel. The Competition (444 bhp) is the sweet spot; the GTS (493 bhp) is the ultimate F82. Budget for a crank hub fix (£1,500–£3,000) or buy one that's already been done.

Our Verdict: The second-generation M4 — bigger, more powerful, and built on the CLAR platform shared with the G80 M3. The S58 is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern performance engine. Available with rear-wheel drive (473 bhp manual) or M xDrive (503 bhp auto). The G82 is the M4 for the modern era — buy it with confidence.
The Engine Choice — The Line That Matters Most
The used sweet spot — budget for crank hub

rows.metric: The Crank Hub Question — The Achilles Heel of the S55
rows.value: Correction applied (internal): The S55 engine in the F82/F83 M4 shares the crank hub weakness with the F80 M3. The crank hub is not keyed to the crankshaft — it relies on friction alone. Under high load (especially on tuned cars), the hub can spin, causing catastrophic piston-to-valve contact. This is the defining risk of the S55-powered M4s. The S58 in the G82 has a revised design that addresses this weakness.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW M4 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW M4 — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: S55 and S58 ratings: 90% confidence — these engines are well-documented across the BMW M range with clear patterns from specialist rebuilders and enthusiast communities. S55 data is drawn from the wider S55 family (shared with M3/M2 Competition). S58 data is pattern-data-tier but consistent with M3/M4 patterns. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The S55 (F82) crank hub is not keyed to the crankshaft — it relies on friction alone. Under high load (especially on tuned cars), the hub can spin, causing piston-to-valve contact. Preventative fix: £1,500–£3,000. Correction applied: The S58 (G82) has a revised crank hub design that addresses the weakness of the S55. The S58 is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern performance engine. Correction applied: The F82 M4 Competition produces 444 bhp (up from the standard 425 bhp). The G82 M4 produces 473 bhp (manual) or 503 bhp (Competition xDrive).

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the M4 range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: The defining F82 M4 issue — the crank hub is not keyed to the crankshaft and relies on friction alone. Under high load (especially on tuned cars), it can spin, causing piston-to-valve contact. Affects F82/F83 M4 (2014–2020). £7,000–£10,000+ recon. Preventative fix: £1,500–£3,000.
The S55's timing chain tensioner can wear, causing rattling on cold start. Skipping oil changes or using the wrong oil accelerates wear. Affects F82/F83 M4.
The rocker cover, oil filter housing and sump gaskets can perish, leading to oil drips. Affects F82/F83 M4. Check for oil drips on any used example.
The S58 engine in the G82 has been reported to consume oil at a higher rate than expected. Some owners require more frequent oil top-ups. Regular oil level monitoring is essential.

The S58's turbochargers and cooling system can experience issues under sustained high-load use. Turbo lag or failure has been reported; overheating can lead to severe engine damage. Regular maintenance is essential.
Not an engine failure — but common non-engine M4 enquiries. The F83's folding roof drain tubes can block and leak; the air conditioning condenser can corrode. Check both on any F83.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW M4 Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — the F82 is a modern classic [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — new generation entering used market [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]

insights: Interesting insight: The M4 generates a different kind of enquiry than the rest of the BMW range — owners are enthusiasts doing due diligence on specific known issues (S55 crank hub) rather than families facing unexpected failures. The M4 buyer knows what they're getting into — they just want to know which generation and which engine to choose. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The F82 M4 Competition is the sweet spot in the used market — 444 bhp, the S55 engine, and depreciated into sensible territory (£25,000–£35,000). The crank hub is the only caveat — budget for a hub fix (£1,500–£3,000) or buy one that's already been done. A 2017+ facelift car with the Competition package is the one to buy. (Editorial synthesis.)

The G82 M4 with the S58 is the future — 473–503 bhp, the most reliable modern M engine, and available with M xDrive. Too new for volume reliability data, but the S58 is proving exceptionally robust in the M3/M4. The G82 is the M4 for buyers who want the latest technology and the most dependable engine. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW M4 Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one M4 based on this page, buy a 2017+ F82 M4 Competition with the S55 — it's the sweet spot in the range: 444 bhp, the LCI updates, and depreciated into sensible territory. Just budget for a crank hub fix (£1,500–£3,000) or buy one that's already been done. If your budget stretches, the G82 with the S58 is the most reliable M4 ever made — and the M xDrive version is devastatingly fast.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW M4 Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW M4 engine typically takes 16–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The M4 rule — pay to play: A £7,000–£10,000+ S55 recon against a £30,000 F82 sits at 23–33% — ✅ safe. Against a £60,000 G82, the same recon is 12–17% — ✅ safe. The M4's values are strong enough to justify engine work — but factor in preventative maintenance (crank hub fix £1,500–£3,000) as part of the purchase cost. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully.

The M4's values are strong enough to justify engine work — but factor in preventative maintenance (crank hub fix £1,500–£3,000) as part of the purchase cost. engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW M4 Engine Range Evolved — 2014 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW launched the M4 as the coupe sibling to the F80 M3, replacing the E92 M3. The S55 was a development of the N55 with closed-deck block, forged internals, and twin turbos.
eras.worthKnowing: The S55 is proven and powerful (425–444 bhp), but the crank hub is the Achilles heel. The Competition (444 bhp) is the sweet spot; the GTS (493 bhp) is the ultimate F82.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The second-generation M4 arrived on the CLAR platform — bigger, more powerful, and with the S58 engine from the G80 M3. The S58 is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern performance engine.
eras.worthKnowing: The S58 is the most reliable M engine ever made. The G82 is available with rear-wheel drive (473 bhp manual) or M xDrive (503 bhp auto). The G82 is the M4 for the modern era — buy it with confidence.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW M4?

profiles.verdict: The M4 is built for this — 425–503 bhp, rear-wheel-drive (or xDrive), and one of the finest coupe chassis in the world. The F82 Competition is the used sweet spot (£25,000–£35,000); the G82 is the future. Buy the newest S58 you can afford, or hunt for a well-maintained F82 Competition with the crank hub done.

The F82 M4 GTS is a future classic — 493 bhp, water injection, and just 803 produced worldwide. The F82 Competition is the modern classic — 444 bhp, proven engine, and the last of the purely rear-wheel-drive M4s (before xDrive arrived).

The M4 is a daily-drivable performance coupe — the F82 is surprisingly practical with a large boot and usable rear seats; the G82 is more refined and comfortable. The F82 is the better weekend car; the G82 is the better daily driver.

The M4 is one of the best track-day cars you can buy — the F82's S55 is proven on track; the G82's S58 with xDrive is devastatingly fast. Budget for cooling upgrades and crank hub prevention on the F82.

The F82 M4 is a modern classic — the Competition is the sweet spot, the GTS is the investment. Both are worth proper investment; values are holding strong. The G82 is the future classic — buy it now and enjoy it.

calculatorCta.h2: Can I Repair My BMW M4 Engine — or Should I Replace It?
calculatorCta.intro: Crank hub spin on an S55? Rattling on cold start from an S55? On the M4, the answer is almost always repair — the values are strong enough to justify engine work. Get a personalised repair-vs-replace verdict using real UK cost data.
